repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 1: shared-edge compatibility makes cellwise bounds nonsharp

published_prior_art.cite: SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 gives sharp bounds for fixed canonical exposure-misspecification settings, not a shared latent sparse-map support frontier
HuLiWager2022 defines direct/indirect effects under specified exposure structure; no joint nonrectangular latent-exposure support theorem
EdmondsMatchingPolytope1965 and Wolsey1975 supply component polyhedral machinery, not the causal endpoint certificate
BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 plus integer-programming certificate machinery cover ingredients, not the combined K-label sparse-network endpoint family

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey still omits the closest hidden-network uncertainty comparators from the adjacent banked proposal, especially Egami2020 and ZhangMohanPearl2023; add them or explain why latent K-support joint direct/indirect geometry is orthogonal.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The certificate statement uses demanded constraints involving Z after only existentially defining t in E_cell(u,P); endpoint rejection is not equivalent to infeasibility of one frozen Z representation unless the quantifier is changed to all cellwise representations or a canonical endpoint witness is fixed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Conjecture 3 by explicitly quantifying over cellwise endpoint witnesses: either prove every representation attaining t has an infeasible hidden-edge system, or define a canonical active-basis witness whose infeasibility certifies only that representation.
Add Egami2020 and ZhangMohanPearl2023 to the related-work comparison around hidden network uncertainty, then state why they do not cover the latent K-support direct/indirect nonrectangularity kernel.

For the witness P*, include the exact finite enumeration plan as a displayed optimization table: hidden-edge subsets, active labels, and the u*=D1-I01 endpoint values needed to certify the 1/40 margin.

recommended_next_step: Revise the Conjecture 3 quantifiers and add the missing hidden-network comparators; the flagship Conjecture 2 kernel can then be accepted if those patches are clean.
